title: Dick Letter on Writing
author: Philip K. Dick
card_summary: A letter by Philip K. Dick concerning the craft or practice of writing. The recipient and precise date are unknown. Five pages. Likely a significant primary-source document for understanding Dick's views on his own literary practice.
page_summary:
A scanned letter by Philip K. Dick concerning the craft or practice of writing. At 5 pages and 1.96 MB, the document is consistent with a multi-page handwritten or typewritten letter. No OCR text layer is present, so the specific content must be determined through direct examination of the scanned image.

Dick was a prolific letter writer whose correspondence frequently touched on the creative process, the economics of the writing life, and the philosophical dimensions of fiction. Letters in which Dick reflects on writing itself are particularly valuable for understanding his literary methods and self-conception as an artist. This document may contain reflections on craft, responses to editorial feedback, or discussions of the relationship between writing and the exploration of reality that characterized his mature work.
